majors.notes: official Harvard page states 50 undergraduate fields of study (concentrations); titles extracted from official page: https://college.harvard.edu/academics/liberal-arts-sciences/concentrations

admissions.testing_policy: Harvard requires SAT or ACT, with alternative exams accepted in exceptional cases when those tests are not accessible.
admissions.gpa_policy: Harvard does not state a simple minimum GPA on the cited first-year page.
admissions.course_rigor: Harvard emphasizes academic accomplishment and broad liberal arts and sciences study, but the cited page does not provide a single minimum course-rigor formula.
admissions.recommendations: Harvard requires a school report that includes a counselor letter and two teacher recommendations.
admissions.essays: Harvard requires a personal essay plus five required short-answer Harvard supplement questions.

projects_research: Harvard says students can participate in hands-on research projects from the moment they arrive.
Harvard admissions says many factors matter beyond academic accomplishment, including special talents and potential contributions to the community.

evidence.claim: Harvard says students can take part in hands-on research projects from the moment they arrive.
evidence.source_excerpt: At Harvard, we put that power into your hands from the moment you arrive. You can take part in hands-on research projects...

evidence.claim: Harvard requires a school report with counselor letter and two teacher recommendations.
evidence.source_excerpt: School Report (which includes a counselor letter) and high school transcript; Teacher Recommendations (2).
We offer more than 3,700 courses in 50 undergraduate fields of study, which we call concentrations.

evidence.claim: Harvard requires SAT or ACT, with alternative exams accepted in exceptional cases when those tests are not accessible.
evidence.source_excerpt: $90 fee ... SAT or ACT - In exceptional cases when those tests are not accessible, one of the following can meet the requirement: AP, IB, GCSE/A-Level, or national leaving exam results.

evidence.claim: Harvard requires a school report that includes a counselor letter and two teacher recommendations.
evidence.source_excerpt: School Report (which includes a counselor letter) and high school transcript; Teacher Recommendations (2).

evidence.claim: Harvard requires a personal essay plus five required short-answer Harvard supplement questions.
evidence.source_excerpt: Personal essay ... There are five required short-answer questions with 150 word limits for each.

evidence.claim: Ranked top 20 professors from RateMyProfessors by rating count and sentiment metrics.
evidence.source_excerpt: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'Harvard University' (legacyId=399) and ranked up to 20 professors with at least one rating.

evidence.claim: Ranked top 20 notable alumni from Wikipedia-derived candidate lists using trailing pageviews.
evidence.source_excerpt: Candidates were harvested from school-specific Wikipedia alumni/people pages or categories, filtered to likely people, and ranked by pageviews. Major-specific and last-10-years filtering requires stronger school-specific alumni sources than this generic pipeline currently has.

warnings: Major titles extracted from official school source (27 titles).
Phase 3 structured upgrade completed with improved majors counting, admissions extraction, and query-ready records.
School-people enrichment uses public third-party sources (RateMyProfessors and Wikipedia/Wikimedia), so rankings are heuristic rather than official university data.

popular_professors.ranking_basis: RateMyProfessors entries sorted by rating_count desc, then average_rating desc, then would_take_again_percent desc.
popular_professors.source_summary: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'Harvard University' (legacyId=399) and ranked up to 20 professors with at least one rating.

successful_alumni.ranking_basis: Curated from official Harvard Alumni, Harvard Gazette, Harvard GSAS, and Harvard SEAS stories, prioritizing person-specific alumni profiles or official university news items with explicit class-year evidence and substantive post-graduation impact summaries.
successful_alumni.source_summary: Extracted from official Harvard alumni and school-domain story/profile pages.

items.name: Maria Perez-Matos
items.bio: Harvard GSAS says Maria Perez-Matos PhD ’25 studies molecular mechanisms of aging and hopes her longevity research will help people live healthier, longer lives.
items.description: Longevity researcher and recent GSAS PhD alumna.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Jonny Kim
items.bio: Harvard Gazette says Jonny Kim, MD ’16 is a NASA astronaut, Harvard-trained physician, and decorated Navy SEAL veteran chosen to address Harvard Alumni Day.
items.description: NASA astronaut, physician, and former Navy SEAL.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Amy Huang
items.bio: Harvard Alumni says Amy Huang ’17 wants to help the world find new ways of feeding itself through work focused on food systems and sustainability.
items.description: Food-systems entrepreneur and sustainability-focused alumna.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Kristen Faulkner
items.bio: Harvard Alumni says cyclist Kristen Faulkner ’16 turned a borrowed bike and a new hobby into Olympic gold.
items.description: Olympic cyclist.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Kirsten Van Fossen
items.bio: Harvard SEAS says Kirsten Van Fossen SB ’12 is using electrochemistry to turn carbon dioxide into usable sustainable fuel.
items.description: Clean-energy entrepreneur working on carbon-to-fuel systems.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Aaron Sabin
items.bio: Harvard GSAS says Aaron Sabin MS/MBA ’23 is developing a practical carbon-capture approach intended to remove billions of tons of CO2 from the atmosphere.
items.description: Climate-tech entrepreneur focused on carbon capture.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Nina Sayles
items.bio: Harvard Gazette says Nina Sayles MPH/MUP ’22 is scaling local agriculture to address nutrition, sustainability, and food insecurity.
items.description: Public-health and urban-planning alumna focused on food systems.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Jacqueline Ashmore
items.bio: Harvard Alumni says Jacqueline Ashmore PhD ’03, executive vice president of engineering at New Leaf Energy, leads development of solar, storage, and wind technologies.
items.description: Clean-energy engineering executive.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Brooke Ellison
items.bio: Harvard Alumni says Brooke Ellison AB ’00, MPP ’04 advocates for disability inclusion and helped launch Harvard’s disability-focused Shared Interest Group.
items.description: Disability-inclusion advocate and public-policy alumna.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Moitri Chowdhury Savard
items.bio: Harvard Gazette says Moitri Chowdhury Savard became Harvard Alumni Association president with a leadership style centered on listening deeply, respecting differences, and finding common ground.
items.description: Harvard Alumni Association president and alumni-community leader.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official university news story with substantive alumni leadership profile, but no explicit class year surfaced in the accessible page metadata used here.

items.name: Tracy Moore II
items.bio: Harvard Gazette says Tracy “Ty” Moore II ’06 entered the Harvard Alumni Association presidency focused on relationship-building, identity, and connection across differences.
items.description: Harvard Alumni Association president and community-builder.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: William Makris
items.bio: Harvard Gazette says William Makris EdM ’00 became incoming HAA board president with a goal of helping alumni share experiences and learn from one another.
items.description: Incoming Harvard Alumni Association board president.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Allyson Mendenhall
items.bio: Harvard Gazette says Allyson Mendenhall AB ’90, MLA ’99 became the first Graduate School of Design alum to serve as HAA president and emphasized inclusive alumni experiences.
items.description: Harvard Alumni Association president and design alumna.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Mary Louise Kelly
items.bio: Harvard Gazette says Mary Louise Kelly ’93 is an award-winning journalist, broadcaster, and author selected as featured speaker for Harvard Alumni Day.
items.description: Journalist, broadcaster, and author.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Sewell Chan
items.bio: Harvard Gazette says distinguished journalist Sewell Chan AB ’98 was chosen by classmates to serve as chief marshal of alumni for his 25th reunion.
items.description: Journalist and alumni chief marshal.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Nozipo Maraire
items.bio: Harvard Alumni says neurosurgeon, author, and green-energy entrepreneur Nozipo Maraire AB ’87 is changing the conversation about climate change and Africa.
items.description: Neurosurgeon, author, and green-energy entrepreneur.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Christian Cooper
items.bio: Harvard Alumni says Christian Cooper ’84 combines birding, storytelling, and science communication and encourages people to ‘nerd out and look up.’
items.description: Naturalist, writer, and science communicator.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Saul Perlmutter
items.bio: Harvard Alumni says Saul Perlmutter ’81 received a Nobel Prize in physics after the middle-of-the-night call that every scientist dreams about.
items.description: Nobel Prize-winning physicist.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Andrew I. Caster
items.bio: Harvard Alumni says Andrew I. Caster AB ’76, MD ’80 established a legacy gift to endow a Harvard Medical School professorship in support of biomedical research.
items.description: Physician and Harvard Medical School benefactor.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Harvard Alumni story with explicit alumni degree years and substantive philanthropic impact details on a shared profile page.

items.name: Jacqueline Jacobs Caster
items.bio: Harvard Alumni says Jacqueline Jacobs Caster MCRP ’83 joined Andrew I. Caster in creating a legacy gift to endow a Harvard Medical School professorship.
items.description: Philanthropist and Harvard benefactor.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Harvard Alumni story with explicit alumni degree year and substantive philanthropic impact details on a shared profile page.
